title: Fix PaymentSuccess page to display correct quantity

requirements.text: Fix the PaymentSuccess page so it correctly reads and displays the quantity selected by the user on the FeaturesPage. The quantity must be passed through the entire flow (FeaturesPage → CheckoutPage → PaymentSuccess) via URL parameters or navigation state, and the PaymentSuccess page must display the actual selected quantity (not a hardcoded value of 1) in the order summary. The total price shown on PaymentSuccess must also reflect quantity × ₹20,000.

quotes: in the last page after the payment is made the quantity doesnt change it is fixed as 1

acceptanceCriteria: When a user selects a quantity greater than 1 on the FeaturesPage and completes the simulated payment flow, the PaymentSuccess page displays that exact quantity in the order summary.
The total price on the PaymentSuccess page equals the selected quantity × ₹20,000.
The quantity value is preserved through CheckoutPage navigation and correctly forwarded to the PaymentSuccess page.
If no quantity is provided (direct navigation), the PaymentSuccess page defaults to quantity 1.
The fix does not break the existing step transitions in the Razorpay-style CheckoutPage flow.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
No real payment processing — all flows remain simulated/dummy.
All images must continue to be served from frontend/public/assets/generated.
nonGoals: Changing the visual design or layout of the PaymentSuccess page beyond the quantity/total fix.
Adding new payment methods or modifying the Razorpay-style checkout UI steps.
